title: MotionRFT: Unified Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Text-to-Motion Generation
abstract: Text-to-motion generation has advanced with diffusion- and flow-based generative models, yet supervised pretraining remains insufficient to align models with high-level objectives such as semantic consistency, realism, and human preference. Existing post-training methods have key limitations: they (1) target a specific motion representation, such as joints, (2) optimize a particular aspect, such as text-motion alignment, and may compromise other factors; and (3) incur substantial computational overhead, data dependence, and coarse-grained optimization. We present a reinforcement fine-tuning framework that comprises a heterogeneous-representation, multi-dimensional reward model, MotionReward, and an efficient, fine-grained fine-tuning method, EasyTune. To obtain a unified semantics representation, MotionReward maps heterogeneous motions into a shared semantic space anchored by text, enabling multidimensional reward learning; Self-refinement Preference Learning further enhances semantics without additional annotations. For efficient and effective fine-tuning, we identify the recursive gradient dependence across denoising steps as the key bottleneck, and propose EasyTune, which optimizes step-wise rather than over the full trajectory, yielding dense, fine-grained, and memory-efficient updates. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of our framework, achieving FID 0.132 at 22.10 GB peak memory for MLD model and saving up to 15.22 GB over DRaFT. It reduces FID by 22.9% on joint-based ACMDM, and achieves a 12.6% R-Precision gain and 23.3% FID improvement on rotation-based HY Motion. Our project page with code is publicly available.
summary:
# 论文总结：MotionRFT: Unified Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Text-to-Motion Generation

## 1. 核心创新点

本文的核心创新，是把 text-to-motion 的后训练从“面向单一动作表示、单一优化目标的局部修补”升级为统一的 reinforcement fine-tuning 框架：用 MotionReward 在 text 锚定的共享语义空间中统一建模异构动作奖励，再用 EasyTune 做去噪步骤级优化。它解决的关键问题是，已有方法很难同时兼顾语义一致性、真实感和偏好对齐，而且通常只适用于某一种 motion representation，并伴随较高训练开销。

## 2. 技术创新拆解

- **创新点 1: MotionReward 的异构表示统一奖励建模**：作者将 joints、rotations 等 heterogeneous motion 映射到同一 text-anchored semantic space，再在该空间里学习多维 reward。这个创新重要在于：它把后训练从“特定表示绑定”的方案，变成了跨表示可复用的统一接口。
- **创新点 2: 多维度 reward 联合优化**：该方法不再只优化 text-motion alignment，而是同时面向 semantic consistency、realism、human preference 等高层目标。它解决了已有方法只盯单一指标、进而牺牲其他质量维度的限制。
- **创新点 3: Self-refinement Preference Learning**：作者通过自我精炼式偏好学习增强语义奖励，而不是额外依赖新的人工偏好标注。它缓解了高质量 preference data 难获取、难扩展的问题，使 reward model 的提升更具可扩展性。
- **创新点 4: EasyTune 的 step-wise 强化微调**：作者把跨去噪步的 recursive gradient dependence 识别为主要瓶颈，并用 step-wise optimization 替代 full-trajectory optimization，从而获得更 dense、fine-grained、memory-efficient 的更新。它带来的直接提升是：强化微调不仅更省显存，而且更适合细粒度地修正生成过程。

## 3. 方法论突破

- **新颖性**：与现有 post-training 方法常见的“只适配一种动作表示 + 只优化一个质量维度 + 沿整条生成轨迹做反传”不同，MotionRFT 同时统一了表示空间、奖励空间和优化粒度。本质上，它不是换了一个 reward，而是重构了 text-to-motion 的后训练范式。
- **关键技术**：核心技术由两部分构成：一是 MotionReward，通过 text-anchored shared semantic space 对 heterogeneous motions 做统一语义表示，并在其上学习多维 reward；二是 EasyTune，通过逐 denoising step 的优化替代整轨迹优化，绕开递归梯度依赖导致的高开销与粗粒度更新问题。
- **理论支撑**：从给定输入可确认，作者对“跨去噪步递归梯度依赖是训练瓶颈”给出了机制性分析，并据此设计 EasyTune；但是否提供了新的严格理论证明、收敛性分析或最优性结论，文中未清楚披露。

## 4. 实验验证

- **主要基准/场景**：当前输入主要包含摘要，未清楚披露具体 benchmark 数据集名称；只能确认作者在 MLD、joint-based ACMDM、rotation-based HY Motion 等模型或实验设置上进行了验证。
- **性能提升**：摘要明确给出的结果包括：在 MLD 上达到 FID 0.132，峰值显存 22.10 GB，并相对 DRaFT 最多节省 15.22 GB；在 joint-based ACMDM 上 FID 降低 22.9%；在 rotation-based HY Motion 上 R-Precision 提升 12.6%，FID 改善 23.3%。
- **消融实验**：当前输入未提供完整消融实验表，因此无法严格判断哪个组件贡献最大；但按作者叙述，MotionReward 主要贡献于跨表示、多目标的性能提升，EasyTune 主要贡献于显存节省和细粒度优化效率。

## 5. 局限与启发

- **当前局限**：从摘要能推断，该框架的效果上限仍高度依赖 reward model 的质量；如果共享语义空间存在偏差，多维 reward 可能会把偏差一并放大。同时，当前输入未清楚披露其在更多骨架拓扑、长时序动作、真实人类偏好评测以及跨数据集泛化上的证据。
- **未来方向**：这项工作开启的方向是：将 text-to-motion 的后训练从监督式补丁升级为通用 RFT 基座，后续可以继续扩展到更强的人类反馈建模、在线偏好优化、视频-文本-动作联合奖励，以及更长时程、更可控的动作生成与编辑。
- **可迁移性**：可迁移性较强。真正可迁移的是“异构结构统一到共享语义空间 + 多维 reward + step-wise RFT”这一方法论，因此理论上可推广到 text-to-video、audio-driven motion、robot trajectory generation 以及其他 diffusion/flow 生成任务；但是否有效，取决于目标领域能否构造可靠的统一语义奖励。

## 6. 一句话总结

如果只能记住一件事，那就是：MotionRFT 通过“异构动作共享语义奖励 + 去噪步骤级强化微调”同时打通了跨表示泛化、多目标对齐和低显存优化三个关键瓶颈，这是它值得关注的技术核心。

recognized_fields.title: MotionRFT: Unified Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Text-to-Motion Generation
recognized_fields.abstract: Text-to-motion generation has advanced with diffusion- and flow-based generative models, yet supervised pretraining remains insufficient to align models with high-level objectives such as semantic consistency, realism, and human preference. Existing post-training methods have key limitations: they (1) target a specific motion representation, such as joints, (2) optimize a particular aspect, such as text-motion alignment, and may compromise other factors; and (3) incur substantial computational overhead, data dependence, and coarse-grained optimization. We present a reinforcement fine-tuning framework that comprises a heterogeneous-representation, multi-dimensional reward model, MotionReward, and an efficient, fine-grained fine-tuning method, EasyTune. To obtain a unified semantics representation, MotionReward maps heterogeneous motions into a shared semantic space anchored by text, enabling multidimensional reward learning; Self-refinement Preference Learning further enhances semantics without additional annotations. For efficient and effective fine-tuning, we identify the recursive gradient dependence across denoising steps as the key bottleneck, and propose EasyTune, which optimizes step-wise rather than over the full trajectory, yielding dense, fine-grained, and memory-efficient updates. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of our framework, achieving FID 0.132 at 22.10 GB peak memory for MLD model and saving up to 15.22 GB over DRaFT. It reduces FID by 22.9% on joint-based ACMDM, and achieves a 12.6% R-Precision gain and 23.3% FID improvement on rotation-based HY Motion. Our project page with code is publicly available.
